meta.title: Solar Panel Soiling Loss Calculator India | MWh & Revenue Impact
meta.description: Free solar soiling loss calculator for Indian utility plants: estimate annual MWh and ₹ lost to dust by MW, soiling %, and tariff. Regional presets for Rajasthan, Gujarat, and typical India.
meta.openGraphDescription: Quantify energy and revenue lost to panel soiling before choosing a cleaning programme — directional tool for IPP and O&M teams.

schema.name: Taypro Solar Panel Soiling Loss Calculator
schema.description: Web calculator estimating annual MWh and revenue loss from solar panel soiling on Indian utility-scale plants.

hero.title: Solar panel soiling loss calculator — how much energy are you losing?
hero.subtitle: Before comparing robot CAPEX or OPEX, quantify the MWh and ₹ foregone to dust. Enter plant MW, soiling loss %, and tariff — uses India-specific yield assumptions (1,500 kWh/kW-year P50). Site-specific results require a soiling study.

prose.heading: Soiling loss is the largest controllable yield leak on Indian utility plants
prose.p1: Soiling suppresses usable output when washing stays calendar-based. Industry discussions cite roughly 8–25% seasonal soiling loss on utility plants in Rajasthan, Gujarat, and other high-dust belts when cleaning is infrequent.
prose.p2: Daily soiling rates of 0.3–0.5% per day are documented in west India dry seasons. Seven days without cleaning can mean 3–5% output loss; ninety days without systematic cleaning can reach 15–25% on desert sites.
prose.p3: This calculator estimates annual generation at your MW capacity, applies your soiling loss assumption, and converts lost MWh to ₹ using your tariff. It does not model cleaning recovery — use the ROI calculator for payback on robotic programmes.
prose.p4: Actual loss depends on tilt, tracker behaviour, rain probability, and dust chemistry. Taypro recommends a site soiling study before binding commercial decisions. See our performance methodology for how we define generation recovery claims.

card0.label: West India dry season
card0.body: Documented daily soiling on arid utility sites — higher after dust storms.

card1.label: Without systematic cleaning
card1.body: Industry range on utility plants when washing cadence is infrequent.

card2.label: 100 MW at 12% loss
card2.body: ~180 GWh annual generation at 1,500 kWh/kW-year — 12% soiling = 21.6 GWh foregone.

card3.label: At ₹3.50/kWh
card3.body: Illustrative annual revenue loss for the 100 MW / 12% example above.

soilingCalculator.heading: Estimate your annual soiling loss
soilingCalculator.subheading: Directional only — not a guarantee of recoverable MWh. Defaults use India market yield (1,500 kWh/kW-year).
soilingCalculator.disclaimer: Results are illustrative. Site-specific soiling studies, PR baselines, and weather normalization are required for investment-grade models. See performance methodology.

soilingTool.soilingHint: share of annual generation lost to dust
soilingTool.tariffHint: PPA, merchant, or blended portfolio rate
soilingTool.disclaimer: Directional estimate only. Does not include cleaning recovery, inverter clipping, or curtailment.

steps.heading: From soiling estimate to cleaning decision
steps.subheading: Four steps O&M teams take after quantifying dust loss.
steps.step0Body: Use regional presets or PR data. High-dust Rajasthan/Gujarat sites often model 15–25%; milder regions 5–12%.
steps.step1Title: Convert MWh to ₹ at your tariff
steps.step1Body: Apply PPA, merchant, or blended ₹/kWh. This is the annual value at risk from dust — not yet net of cleaning cost.
steps.step2Body: Run the ROI calculator with CAPEX or OPEX. Compare recovered MWh value vs cleaning TCO over 5 years.
steps.step3Body: Taypro engineers assess dust sources, cycle count, and robot model before binding commercial terms.
steps.step4Body: Correlate cleaning cycles with block-level PR trends — closing the SCADA blind spot on field action.

highlight.heading: The O&M question
highlight.lead: Do you know your annual ₹ at risk from dust?
highlight.body: Many plants model inverter availability but not soiling loss in ₹. This calculator gives a directional starting point before RFQ and ROI modelling.

proseSecondary.heading: Soiling chemistry varies by Indian state
proseSecondary.p1: Rajasthan and Gujarat see loose silica and cementitious films — dry-brush robotic cleaning fits well. Coastal Tamil Nadu and Andhra Pradesh add salt aerosols. Metro-adjacent plants face soot and PM2.5 with different adhesion.
proseSecondary.p2: Agri-adjacent sites see harvest dust spikes in October–November. Monsoon rain can reset modules — but pre-monsoon dust seasons often drive the highest annual loss.
proseSecondary.p3: Weekly manual cleaning may leave 3–6% residual annual loss; optimised daily robotic programmes target under 2% in field discussions — always site-dependent.

faq.heading: Solar soiling loss calculator — FAQ
faq.subheading: How to interpret results and next steps.
faq.a0: Plant MW × 1,000 kW × specific yield (default 1,500 kWh/kW-year for India P50). Adjust tariff; yield is held constant unless you request a site-specific study.
faq.a1: Use PR history or regional benchmarks: 5–8% mild, 8–15% typical utility India, 15–25% high-dust Rajasthan/Gujarat without frequent cleaning.
faq.q2: Does this show recoverable energy after cleaning?
faq.a2: No. It estimates loss from dust only. Use the ROI calculator to model cleaning cost vs recovered MWh.
faq.q3: Is this the same as the robot price calculator?
faq.a3: No. This tool quantifies soiling loss. The price calculator models robot CAPEX/OPEX payback over 20 years.
faq.q4: How does soiling relate to PR?
faq.a4: Soiling suppresses PR. SCADA shows kWh after loss; this calculator translates assumed soiling % into MWh and ₹ at risk.
faq.a5: When soiling loss is under ~1.5–2% and cleaning cost exceeds recovered PPA value — common on mild sites or during heavy rain seasons.
faq.a6: Use your effective ₹/kWh: PPA rate, merchant capture, or blended portfolio average.
faq.a7: Yes, but yield assumptions differ for rooftop. Utility ground-mount defaults are most accurate above ~1 MW.
faq.q8: How often should modules be cleaned in Rajasthan?
faq.a8: Many utility programmes run 3–10 dry cycles monthly in dry season. A soiling study sets the right cadence.
faq.a9: See /performance-and-test-methodology for how Taypro defines dust removal and generation recovery claims.
faq.a10: Yes. Request a site soiling study and engineering review before binding cleaning contracts.
faq.a11: Lost MWh from soiling reduces avoided emissions. See our carbon/soiling blog for portfolio framing.

cta.heading: Know your loss — then model the fix
cta.body: Use the ROI calculator to compare cleaning programme economics, or contact Taypro for a site soiling study.
